portifolio.title: Portfolio
portifolio.name: Diogo Medeiros
portifolio.description: Full-Stack Developer and Software Engineering student. With 6 years of experience in game localization, I bring international professional maturity and a rigorous eye for detail to software development.
project.description.1:
Interactive narrative web game set in a chaotic corporate environment.

Built with React, TypeScript, Tailwind, and Brawlify API.

A fan-made academic tribute to the Brawl Stars universe built with React and TypeScript.
about.title: Multicultural Peer
about.description:
Software Engineering student with six years of experience in game localization, now applied to building robust software systems. Notably, I spent two years working on-site in Dublin with industry-leading developers: Bungie and Supercell.

Being part of multicultural teams and working on products that impact millions of users daily taught me to meet rigorous technical requirements and deliver high-quality solutions with an end-user-first mindset.

Today, I approach fullstack development with the same commitment to quality, focusing on solid foundations, code clarity, architectural implications, and long-term maintainability.

My tech stack includes React, TypeScript, Vite, and Tailwind for the frontend, and Python, C#, and Java for the backend. I am seeking opportunities in engineering teams that prioritize technical excellence and critical problem-solving.

blog.page.title: Blog
blog.post.1.title: Building Starr Corp: Office Mayhem - Behind the Scenes
blog.post.1.excerpt: An in-depth look at how I designed the logical engine and scene manager for my Brawl Stars tribute project.
blog.post.1.intro: Developing a Strategy Web Game in React and TypeScript
blog.post.1.p1: Creating Starr Corp: Office Mayhem was a great exercise in designing modular game logic within React's render loop. The core goal was to separate simulation rules from components, achieving clean, predictable updates.
blog.post.1.p2: In this article, I discuss the architecture behind the scene manager and custom engine hooks, showing how they manage brawler stats, choices, and office chaos levels without unnecessary re-renders.
blog.post.1.p3: You can find more detail in the full project documentation using the link below, or check out the code directly in the GitHub repository.
